task: Disable hardcoded gates bypassing tuning_config in trading_cycle.py
local_output: Found that gates were blocking trades but didn't know which were hardcoded vs configurable
opus_correction: Vault search confirmed 'Gates stay OFF' from Apr 6 and Apr 8 decisions. Identified 6 specific hardcoded gates (momentum_trap, hard_oscillator_exhaustion, against_momentum, oscillator_freshness, oscillator_direction, bb_width) that bypass tc_get() entirely. Converted each to log-only 'WOULD_BLOCK (disabled)' while preserving 10 safety/structural gates (session, cooldown, open trade guard, news, candle fetch, direction conflicts, EMA21 wrong-side). Key distinction: entry-quality filters vs structural guardrails.
opus_reasoning: The root cause was that 6 gates used raw RSI/Stoch thresholds instead of tc_get() calls. The fix required classifying each gate as entry-quality (disable) vs safety/structural (keep). Vault provided the decision authority — 300 optimizer trials with gates ON scored 0.0, confirming gates should stay off. Log-only approach preserves observability without blocking.

task: Analyze SL-killed trades and compute EMA-aware structural SL savings
local_output: Knew losing trades existed but hadn't quantified which were SL-killed vs guardian-exited or whether structural SL would help
opus_correction: Separated 31 losers into 10 SL-killed (hard stop fired) and 19 guardian-managed (threat scoring exit). For the 10 SL-killed, pulled M15 OANDA candle data, computed EMA100 at entry, derived structural SL = max(3xATR, abs(entry - E100) + buffer). Result: 9/10 would survive, saving 171.8 pips / $798.91. 6 saved by structural floor (E100 far from entry), 3 by ATR-3x floor (old SL used stale 1.5x multiplier). One trade not saved — exit exactly at 3xATR boundary.
opus_reasoning: The analysis required: 1) correct trade categorization (SL-killed vs guardian), 2) OANDA API integration for candle data (token encrypted in DB, found in .env), 3) EMA100 computation at trade entry time, 4) comparison of old vs new SL methodology. The structural SL formula max(3xATR, structural_floor) provides a dynamic minimum that respects both volatility and price structure.
